idea-plugin.name: Truth Instruction Set (TIS)
idea-plugin.description:
Language support for TIS (Truth Instruction Set) — a composition description language at L0 of the GTC/CPT/PMCA/Application stack where degeneracy is grammatically inexpressible. Derived from the General Theory of Composition.

Features

  • Syntax highlighting for .tis files with four primitive type categories: Entity (noun), Condition (adjective), Coupling (verb), Scope (boundary)
  • Zero-determinant error detection (E302): det: 0.0 flagged at the lexer level — zero determinants are type errors, not runtime exceptions
  • Context-sensitive condition ordering (E100): C1-C2-C3-C4 acquisition ordering enforced — condition validity depends on prior declarations
  • Compositional completeness check (E200): d_C = 4 requires all four conditions
  • Chain continuity check (E301): link[i].output must equal link[i+1].input
  • Chain product computation: product of det(J_i) accumulated in real-time (ACCUMULATE is the parsing process, not a document element)
  • Phase sum check (E401): sum of phases exceeding pi = destructive interference
  • Orphan detection (E303): conditions and metadata without coupling declarations
  • Condition number warning (E500): kappa exceeds threshold = fragile composition
  • ground_truth position enforcement (E100): must appear first (highest attention weight)
  • Comment toggling with #
  • Color customization: Settings > Editor > Color Scheme > Truth Instruction Set

What is TIS?

TIS is not a data format with validation bolted on. It is a composition description language where the grammar enforces non-degeneracy — degenerate compositions are syntax errors, not runtime exceptions. Expression IS verification.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.1.0 — Structural annotator: condition ordering, d_C completeness, chain continuity, chain product computation, accumulated phase sum, orphan detection, metadata coupling, ground_truth position enforcement.

1.0.0 — Initial release. Syntax highlighting, zero-det annotator, file type registration.
